message:
perf(step_size): inline RMS norm to eliminate temporary vector allocations

The initial_step_size computation previously allocated three temporary
Vec<f64> (scaled_y0, scaled_f0, diff_scaled) solely to pass them to
rms_norm(). This refactor fuses the scaling and sum-of-squares
accumulation into single passes over the data, removing the standalone
rms_norm() helper entirely. The numerical result is identical -- only
the allocation pattern changes.

For high-dimensional ODE systems this reduces per-call heap pressure by
3*n f64s, which matters in hot integration loops where initial_step_size
is evaluated at every restart.

Also update .gitignore to preserve the P2C-001 perf profile report
artifact (perf_profile_report.json) while still ignoring other generated
conformance artifacts in that directory, following the same pattern
already used for P2C-007.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,6 +39,10 @@ target_*/
 
 # Conformance test output artifacts (generated per-run)
 crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/*
+!crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/P2C-001/
+crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/P2C-001/*
+!crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/P2C-001/perf/
+!crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/P2C-001/perf/perf_profile_report.json
 !crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/P2C-007/
 crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/P2C-007/*
 !crates/fsci-conformance/fixtures/artifacts/P2C-007/e2e/
